answer:
Honestly, one activity I really enjoy doing for fun is playing basketball. I’ve been into it since I was around twelve, but I guess it’s become a bigger part of my life in recent years, especially as I’ve started preparing to apply to universities in the States. At first, I was honestly pretty clumsy—you know, I’d miss basic shots and get exhausted after just a few minutes on the court. But over time, I started dedicating much more effort to improving. For example, I spent a lot of time watching tutorials online and practicing dribbling and shooting technique, which actually made a huge difference. Now, I think my game is a lot more strategic and my stamina has improved a lot, so I can play longer and be more competitive.

I usually play with a group of close friends—sometimes even organizing small tournaments at school. Playing together definitely brought us closer, because you have to communicate really well and trust each other on the court. I mean, there have been moments when we disagreed about a play or lost a match, but those experiences taught us how to resolve conflicts and support each other. It kind of strengthened our bond and made our friendship more resilient.

Overall, I find that basketball isn’t just fun—it’s helped me develop discipline and teamwork, and honestly, it’s shaped the way I approach challenges both on and off the court. So, yeah, I really value the impact it’s had on my relationships and personal growth.
title: What is an activity you do for fun? How have you gotten better at this activity over time? Who do you enjoy doing this activity with, and how has it impacted your relationship?
